---
title: "Basemap for NYC"
author: "James McQuilkin"
date: "2026-04-30"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

#Load Packages
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(sf)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
```

#Generate Basic Choropleth Map Colored by Borough
```{r}
# Import the shapefile, transform to NYC State Plane
nynta <- read_sf(here("data/raw_data/mapping_data/nynta2020_25d/nynta2020.shp")) %>% 
  st_transform(2263)

#Plot map
ggplot(data = nynta) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = BoroName), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  labs(
    title = "NYC Neighborhood Tabulation Areas By Borough (2020)",
    fill = "Borough",
    caption = "Source: NYC Open Data"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

#Creating a Theoretical Grid for Analysis
```{r}
# 1. Create a hexagonal grid across the entire extent of the NYC layer
grid_map <- nynta %>% 
  st_make_grid(cellsize = 600, square = FALSE) %>% 
  st_sf()

# 2. Clip the grid to the NYC coastline
# We use st_union(nynta) to dissolve borough lines so hexes only clip at the exterior edge
grid_map_clipped <- st_intersection(grid_map, st_union(nynta))

# 3. Final Visualization
ggplot() + 
  geom_sf(data = nynta_proj, aes(fill = BoroName), color = "white", alpha = 0.7) + 
  geom_sf(data = grid_map_clipped, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.05) +
  labs(
    title = "NYC Hexagonal Analysis Grid",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    fill = "Borough"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom", 
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed")
  )
```
